data.title: Pancreatic cancer survival rates improving with novel therapies like antibody treatment; immune system targets tumors.
data.description: Oncolytics Biotech Inc., based in Vancouver, BC, announced on December 12, 2025, the development of an antibody treatment that aims to enhance the immune system's ability to target pancreatic tumors. This treatment works by obstructing the sugar-coated shields that cancer cells typically utilize to avoid detection. The breakthrough signifies a potential shift in pancreatic cancer treatment approaches, offering renewed hope for patients.

data.title: Liverpool LTC workers at Queens Manor Nursing home voted 100% in favor of a strike due to low wages and understaffing. CUPE 2648 members are at their breaking point.
data.description: In Liverpool, Nova Scotia, long-term care employees at Queens Manor Nursing Home, who are members of Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) 2648, have unanimously voted in favor of a strike mandate. The president of CUPE 2648, Peter Howard, expressed mixed feelings about the outcome, stating that while nobody desires a strike, the workers are facing significant challenges. The vote indicates a 100% support for strike action due to concerns over inadequate wages that do not align with the cost of living and the ongoing issue of understaffing.

data.title: Mission Space will launch its second payload with Rogue Space at the Spacepower Conference in Orlando, with an expanding in-situ measurement network. (characters: 128)
data.description: In Orlando, Florida on December 12, 2025, Mission Space, a leading commercial space-weather company known for its flight experience and growing in-situ measurement network, revealed plans to launch its second payload in collaboration with Rogue Space. The announcement took place at the Spacepower Conference, emphasizing Mission Space's unique position in the space industry.

data.title: HTX, a global digital asset exchange with 50M users, now approved for registration in Pakistan's crypto exchange market.
data.description: In Islamabad, Pakistan on December 12, 2025, it was reported that HTX, a prominent digital asset exchange company with a user base exceeding 50 million worldwide, has successfully acquired a No Objection Certificate (NoC) from the Pakistan Virtual Asset Regulation Authority.

data.title: IEA cuts 2026 oil surplus forecast to 3.84 million bpd from 4.09 million bpd due to strong demand and weak supply amid sanctions. Oil prices rise.
data.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has revised its forecast for the global oil surplus in 2026, expecting a drop to 3.84 million barrels per day, down from the previous estimate of 4.09 million bpd. This adjustment is attributed to stronger demand expectations and decreased supply from countries facing sanctions. Oil prices saw an increase, with Brent crude futures reaching $61.75 per barrel and U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude at $58.09 per barrel. Despite this, both benchmarks were still on track for a weekly loss of 3%. The Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund rose by 0.48%, including ExxonMobil Corp and Chevron Corp, while the United States Oil Fund gained 0.45%. The IEA also improved its global oil demand growth forecasts for both 2025 and 2026 due to an improving economy and easing tariff concerns.

data.title: Reddit is suing Australia over a social media ban for under-16s, claiming a violation of free speech. (Numbers: 16, Reddit, Australia, SMMA)
data.description: Reddit (NASDAQ: RDDT) is challenging Australia's ban on social media for under-16s, arguing that it hampers free political discourse. In a high-stakes legal battle, Reddit has taken the issue to Australia's highest court, contesting the new Social Media Minimum Age (SMMA) law that was put into effect just two days prior. This ban marks a global first and impacts Reddit, a prominent platform in Australia. While Reddit is complying with the law, the company asserts that the ban breaches the country's constitutional right to free political communication. Reddit argues for an exemption from the ban, claiming it does not fall under the category of social media. The platform acknowledges the importance of safeguarding users under 16 but suggests that the law is overly intrusive. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kevin O'Leary argues Supreme Court shouldn't limit Trump's tariff powers, says presidential control is necessary for trade policy.
data.description: Kevin O'Leary, a prominent investor, recently expressed his opinion on the speculation surrounding President Donald Trump's authority to impose tariffs. O'Leary argued against the idea that the Supreme Court could limit Trump's tariff powers, stating that the president's control over trade policy has been a longstanding and essential element. During an appearance on Fox Business' "Mornings With Maria," O'Leary emphasized the necessity of the executive branch having the authority to set trade policies, regardless of who holds the office. While acknowledging that debates can arise over specific tariff rates, O'Leary cautioned that completely revoking this power would establish a dangerous precedent. He highlighted the potential for such an action to disrupt global trade and hinder effective responses to economic threats. O'Leary's remarks underscore the significance of maintaining presidential authority over trade policy for ensuring stability and resilience in the face of economic challenges.
